name: Allen Brain Observatory — Visual Coding (2-Photon)
description: The largest publicly available standardized survey of in-vivo two-photon calcium imaging from awake, head-fixed mice, recording ~60,000 cells across six visual cortical areas, multiple layers and 13 Cre lines during a battery of visual stimuli including natural movies. While not itself a social paradigm, it is a flagship open neurophysiology resource and a model for standardized data sharing in systems neuroscience.
citation: de Vries SEJ, Lecoq JA, Buice MA, et al. (2020). A large-scale standardized physiological survey reveals functional organization of the mouse visual cortex. Nature Neuroscience, 23, 138–151.

publications.title: Sharing neurophysiology data from the Allen Brain Observatory
